{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-49834", "cvss": {"score": 5.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-345", "name": "Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity"}], "description": "### Impact
_What kind of vulnerability is it? Who is impacted?_

A verifier configured with WithTransparencyLog(N>1) or WithSignedCertificateTimestamps(N>1) expected defense-in-depth against the compromise of a single log instance. However, threshold counting counted verified witnesses per-entry or per-validation-path rather than per-log-authority.

As a result, a single compromised transparency log could forge multiple entries with different indices, and a single compromised CT log could verify multiple times (either across multiple certificate chains or via multiple embedded SCTs), fully satisfying the multi-log threshold requirements and defeating the multi-log policy.

Note that this does not affect Cosign, as Cosign sets a threshold of 1.

### Patches
_Has the problem been patched? What versions should users upgrade to?_

Upgrade to v1.1.5.

### Workarounds
_Is there a way for users to fix or remediate the vulnerability without upgrading?_

There is no workaround, beyond relying on trusted logs.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00169, "percentile": 0.06578}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-9vcr-p3rj-q5q6", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-09T23:20:30Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9vcr-p3rj-q5q6", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-9vcr-p3rj-q5q6"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-49834"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-09T23:20:30Z", "references": ["https://github.com/sigstore/sigstore-go/security/advisories/GHSA-9vcr-p3rj-q5q6", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9vcr-p3rj-q5q6"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/sigstore/sigstore-go/security-advisories/GHSA-9vcr-p3rj-q5q6", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/sigstore/sigstore-go", "summary": "sigstore-go has a multi-log threshold bypass via single compromised log", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-09T23:20:30Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-9vcr-p3rj-q5q6", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.2.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/sigstore/sigstore-go"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 1.1.4"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: sigstore-go has a multi-log threshold bypass via single compromised log
